hypothesis: On XAUUSD M5, a tight Asia range (range/ATR in [0.4, 1.6]) followed by a London-open displacement candle (body >= 1.1*ATR(14)) closing beyond the Asia extreme by >= 0.4*ATR signals genuine vol expansion that continues for ~12-18 bars. Entry at market on breakout bar close; ATR-scaled stops avoid the LVC-EA-style 'never fires' bug by using close-based breakout rather than intrabar touch.

entry_rules.precondition: asia_range_width / ATR(14) between 0.4 and 1.6, measured at 06:00 UTC
entry_rules.long: london_trade==1 AND close > asia_high + 0.4*ATR AND (close-open) >= 1.1*ATR AND close > ema50
entry_rules.short: london_trade==1 AND close < asia_low - 0.4*ATR AND (open-close) >= 1.1*ATR AND close < ema50

regime_filter.rationale: Need some baseline volatility and at least mild directional bias on H1.

notes: Rewrite of prior zero-trade Asia-London spec. Fix: close-based (not touch) breakout, explicit broker-UTC conversion handled in session_mask primitive, EMA50 trend gate, one trade per day cap. Inspired by inspiration_only LVC-EA card.
